metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename the criterion prompt file from `code-review-project.review-phase-boundaries.md` to `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md` (dot→dash, matching `_valid_id`'s `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*` rule and the plan-review `ids.py` precedent) and update all referencing file_impact entries and AC verify commands to use the dash form.
evidence: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/prompt_authoring.py:50`: `_SLUG = re.compile(r'^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*$')` — dots are excluded
`src/rebar/llm/prompting/prompt_library.py:187`: `if not _valid_id(prompt_id): ...` — load path validates the id
Epic file_impact entry: `{"path": ".rebar/prompts/code-review-project.review-phase-boundaries.md", ...}`
Plan-review analog in `ids.py`: `project.foo` → `plan-review-project-foo` (the single namespace dot becomes a dash); the code-review equivalent would be `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries`
findings.finding: The plan names the project criterion's prompt file as `code-review-project.review-phase-boundaries.md` (containing a dot), but the runtime prompt-id validator (`_valid_id` = `[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9-]*`) rejects any id containing a dot, causing `get_prompt()` to raise an error when the criterion's prompt is loaded.
findings.location: Epic file_impact: `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project.review-phase-boundaries.md`; criterion story `225a-323c-a9f6-436b` file_impact same entry; `ids.py` module docstring establishes the plan-review analog is `plan-review-project-<name>` (dot→dash).
scenarios: When `CodeReviewBatchRunner` fans the activated project criterion into Pass 1 and calls `get_prompt('code-review-project.review-phase-boundaries', ...)`, the prompt library rejects the dotted id and the criterion silently fails or raises, blocking the full end-to-end flow the seam story promises.
The eval resolver, once extended to recognize project code-review criteria, would also attempt to load this dot-containing id and fail the same validation.
findings.suggested_fix: In `ids.py` (or a new code-review analog), define the code-review project criterion prompt-id mapping as `project.<name>` → `code-review-project-<name>` (single dot replaced by a dash, same rule as plan-review). Update the file_impact entries in the epic and criterion story to `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.md`, the eval spec to `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries.eval.yaml`, and the AC routing check to reference the dash form.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py` to bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8`'s repair scope (or story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02`'s file_impact) so the `_criteria_eval` pre-flight guard is updated to also accept activated code-review project criteria (e.g. by consulting `code_review.registry.effective_criteria(repo_root)` in addition to `plan_review.registry.by_id(repo_root)`).
evidence: `_criteria_eval` at `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py:147`: `if args.criterion_id not in registry.by_id(repo_root):` — uses `plan_review.registry`, not `code_review.registry`
Bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` file_impact lists `ids.py`, `batch_runner.py`, `workflow_ops.py`, `eval_solver.py` — `_llm_eval_commands.py` is ABSENT
Story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` file_impact similarly omits `_llm_eval_commands.py`
Epic-level AC specifies: `.venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json` — this command path hits the plan-review guard before any eval_solver code

`eval_solver._code_review_prompt_id` (line 162-176) also doesn't handle project criteria, but that is correctly identified and scoped to `eval_solver.py` in the bug ticket; the CLI guard is the additional unscoped gap
findings.finding: The `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries` command will fail at the CLI pre-flight guard in `_llm_eval_commands.py` (line 147) even after bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` is fixed. That guard checks `plan_review.registry.by_id(repo_root)`, which only knows plan-review criteria — a code-review-only project criterion will never appear there and the command returns exit-1 with 'unknown criterion' before reaching `eval_solver`. Neither the bug ticket's `file_impact` list nor the parent story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` names `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py` as a repair seam, leaving this blocking CLI-layer gap unaddressed.
findings.location: Scope item 1 (seam story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02`) / bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` file_impact list / epic-level AC: `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json`
scenarios: Developer runs `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json` after all child stories close; gets exit-1 'unknown criterion' from the CLI guard rather than running calibration fixtures.
The epic-level AC check fails at the CLI layer despite the eval_solver seam being correctly repaired, blocking epic closure.
findings.suggested_fix: In `_criteria_eval`, extend the pre-flight guard to check the code-review effective criteria when the plan-review registry lookup misses: `from rebar.llm.code_review.registry import effective_criteria as cr_criteria; if args.criterion_id not in registry.by_id(repo_root) and args.criterion_id not in cr_criteria(repo_root): ...`

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the gate-aware prompt-id mapping rule for code-review project criteria (e.g., `code-review-project-<name>`) in bug `8a4b` / story `2c14`, and add the corresponding constant to `ids.py` so the seam and eval resolver agree on the physical file name.
evidence: `criterion_prompt_id` always returns `f"{PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX}{criterion_id.replace('.', '-')}"` — the prefix is `plan-review-` unconditionally.
Epic file_impact lists the prompt at `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project.review-phase-boundaries.md`, implying prefix `code-review-` not `plan-review-`.
Bug scope says 'gate-aware, collision-safe logical criterion-id to prompt-id mapping' but gives no rule for what the new mapping looks like.
No `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX` constant exists in `ids.py` or anywhere in the codebase.
findings.finding: The `criterion_prompt_id()` function in `ids.py` hard-codes a `plan-review-` prefix for ALL project criteria, but `project.review-phase-boundaries` is a code-review criterion whose prompt file the plan names `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project.review-phase-boundaries.md`. No gate-aware mapping rule (e.g., a `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX` constant or a gate parameter) is specified anywhere in the plan or child tickets — an implementer of bug `8a4b` must invent the new mapping rule without a spec.
findings.location: Bug 8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8 — Scope / Implementation Notes; epic file_impact `.rebar/prompts/code-review-project.review-phase-boundaries.md`; `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py`
scenarios: Bug implementer defaults to `plan-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` as the prompt-id, prompts resolve from wrong path, criterion silently never fires.
Story 225a author uses `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries` but eval_solver's `_code_review_prompt_id` rejects it (closed set), causing eval command to raise ValueError.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = "code-review-"` to `ids.py` and define `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, *, gate='plan_review')` that dispatches on gate, emitting `code-review-project-<name>` for code-review project criteria. Document this rule explicitly in both `8a4b` and `2c14`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the eval interpretation rule for AC 3's `--runs 3` check (e.g., 'all 3 runs must flag every RP-F case and emit no finding for every RP-N case' or 'the tool's built-in aggregate score must reach 100%').
evidence: `.venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json`
'emits findings for RP-F1..RP-F6, and emits none for RP-N1..RP-N6' — no per-run vs aggregate rule stated
LLM-backed evals are inherently probabilistic; 3 runs may not all agree
findings.finding: AC 3's `rebar criteria eval` command uses `--runs 3` on an LLM-backed eval but provides no stated interpretation rule for what constitutes 'pass' across those runs — the phrase 'emits findings for RP-F1..RP-F6 and emits none for RP-N1..RP-N6' does not specify whether all 3 runs must produce perfect results, a majority suffices, or the tool applies a built-in threshold. An executor cannot determine pass/fail without this rule.
scenarios: Executor runs eval and gets 2/3 runs flagging all F cases — unclear if this is a pass or fail
Two N-cases slip through on one run — no stated tolerance level
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to AC 3: 'All three runs must flag every RP-F1..RP-F6 case and produce zero findings for every RP-N1..RP-N6 case; a single miss on any run is a failure.' If the eval tool already aggregates and reports a pass/fail threshold, cite that instead.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact target file for 225a's 'dogfood documentation subsection' in AC-5, confirming it is distinct from docs/llm-framework.md (e.g., '.rebar/README.md' or 'docs/dogfood-log.md'), or explicitly state that 225a appends a named subsection to docs/llm-framework.md after 2c14's section lands.
evidence: 225a AC-5: 'its implementation diff changes only committed .rebar/ prompt/routing/eval assets, their .gitignore exceptions, criterion-specific tests, and a dogfood documentation subsection'
2c14 Scope: 'Update docs/llm-framework.md to describe code-review project-criterion runtime resolution, configuration failures, and the eval entry point, completing rather than replacing ADR 0017'
The target file for 225a's 'dogfood documentation subsection' is not named, leaving open whether it is docs/llm-framework.md (owned by 2c14), a .rebar/ README, or a new file
container pairing: parent + 225a-323c-a9f6-436b/2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02/9447-b31c-e0b7-446d
findings.finding: Child 225a AC-5 states its implementation diff 'changes only committed .rebar/ prompt/routing/eval assets, their .gitignore exceptions, criterion-specific tests, and a dogfood documentation subsection', but child 2c14 also owns documentation updates to docs/llm-framework.md covering the code-review project-criterion runtime. The term 'dogfood documentation subsection' in 225a is undefined and could refer to a subsection of docs/llm-framework.md, creating an undemarcated documentation scope overlap between the two children.
scenarios: 225a's implementer adds a dogfood subsection to docs/llm-framework.md; 2c14's implementer has already updated that file; a merge conflict or duplication arises
The completion verifier for 225a AC-5 cannot determine whether the 'dogfood documentation subsection' change is within the allowed scope or constitutes an undeclared core-file change
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'a dogfood documentation subsection' in 225a AC-5 with a named file reference, e.g. 'a .rebar/DOGFOOD.md entry documenting the project.review-phase-boundaries criterion's purpose and eval results'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align 9447 AC-2 with parent AC-4 by either explicitly requiring Pass-2/Pass-3 evidence for the positive control (even if vacuous — showing zero findings entered Pass 2) or amending parent AC-4 to restrict the Pass-2/Pass-3 requirement to the negative probe only.
evidence: Parent AC-4: 'capture a valid positive control and an invalid cross-phase directive in real advisory-only reviews, including Pass-2 and Pass-3 evidence'

9447 AC-2: 'A positive-control Gerrit patchset is recorded with change id, patchset SHA, UTC timestamp, and review artifact proving the project criterion ran and emitted no phase boundary finding for a valid discovery/evidence refinement' — no Pass-2 or Pass-3 fields required
9447 AC-3 (negative probe) does include Pass-2 and Pass-3 fields, confirming the asymmetry is intentional in the child but not sanctioned by the parent
container pairing: parent + 225a-323c-a9f6-436b/2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02/9447-b31c-e0b7-446d
findings.finding: Child 9447 AC-2 (positive control) requires only 'change id, patchset SHA, UTC timestamp, and review artifact proving the project criterion ran and emitted no phase boundary finding' — it does not require Pass-2 or Pass-3 evidence for the positive control patchset. Parent AC-4 says 'capture a valid positive control and an invalid cross-phase directive in real advisory-only reviews, including Pass-2 and Pass-3 evidence', which as written applies to both reviews, not only the negative probe. This is a scope-narrowing of the positive control's evidence requirements.
scenarios: Completion verifier checks parent AC-4 and finds no Pass-2/Pass-3 data recorded for the positive control patchset; 9447 is deemed incomplete even though its own AC-2 was satisfied

If the positive control unexpectedly produces findings, there is no requirement to record how those findings were handled in Pass-2/Pass-3, hiding a potential false-positive from the calibration record
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to parent AC-4 clarifying scope, e.g. 'including Pass-2 and Pass-3 evidence for the negative probe', OR add a field to 9447 AC-2 such as 'and Pass-3 output confirming zero findings entered the decision phase'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or constrain the rubric-change exception in 9447's out-of-scope: either delete it entirely (defect correction requires a new ticket citing probe evidence) or narrow it to 'no rubric changes; defects found during dogfood are recorded and resolved via a follow-on ticket'.
evidence: 9447 Out of scope: 'changing the rubric except to correct a defect demonstrated by the probe'

Parent scope section for 9447 (item 3): 'capture a valid positive control and an invalid cross-phase directive in real advisory-only reviews, including Pass-2 and Pass-3 evidence; amend the invalid directive out before submission'

Parent out-of-scope: 'tuning the 0.90 threshold without real-review data, changing the kernel's impact model or deterministic decision math, modifying packaged built-in criteria' — no rubric-change exception
225a owns and closes the criterion prompt/eval assets; 9447 has no parent-sanctioned write authority over those assets
container pairing: parent + 225a-323c-a9f6-436b/2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02/9447-b31c-e0b7-446d
findings.finding: Child 9447 includes an exception in its out-of-scope section — 'changing the rubric except to correct a defect demonstrated by the probe' — that grants 9447 implicit authority to modify .rebar/ criterion assets authored and eval-corpus-validated by 225a. The parent plan gives no such authority to 9447; the parent's scope for 9447 is purely observational, and out-of-scope items include 'changing the kernel's impact model' and 'tuning the 0.90 threshold without real-review data', with no rubric-change exception mentioned.
scenarios: If the live probe reveals a false positive in the criterion prompt, 9447 modifies the prompt and adds a new eval case without re-running the full twelve-case corpus mandated by 225a's AC-3, leaving parent AC-3 in a potentially invalid state
The rubric change in 9447 would not be covered by 225a's operator-attested eval AC-4 (three-run live eval recorded on 225a's ticket), creating a criterion whose live eval attestation is stale
A future reviewer cannot determine which story owns the authoritative version of the criterion prompt — 225a or 9447
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '9447 Out of scope: changing the rubric except to correct a defect demonstrated by the probe' with 'changing the criterion prompt or eval corpus — any defect surfaced by the probe is recorded on this ticket and addressed in a follow-on story that re-runs the full eval attestation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile 225a AC-1's verification command with the schema assumed by parent AC-5 and 9447 AC-1: either add the `activate` key to the shared schema definition and update all verification commands, or remove the `d["activate"]` check from 225a AC-1 if activation is implied by presence in the `code_review` block.
evidence: 225a AC-1 assertion: `assert "project.review-phase-boundaries" in d["activate"] and r["exec"] == "AGENT" and r["block_threshold"] == 0.90 and r["blocking_enabled"] is False`

Parent AC-5 assertion: `r=json.load(open(".rebar/criteria_routing.json"))["code_review"]["project.review-phase-boundaries"]; assert r["block_threshold"] == 0.90 and r["blocking_enabled"] is False` — no `activate` key

9447 AC-1 assertion: `r=json.load(open(".rebar/criteria_routing.json"))["code_review"]["project.review-phase-boundaries"]; assert r["block_threshold"] == 0.90 and r["blocking_enabled"] is False` — no `activate` key
225a also checks `r["exec"] == "AGENT"` which is not verified by parent or 9447
container pairing: parent + 225a-323c-a9f6-436b/2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02/9447-b31c-e0b7-446d
findings.finding: Child 225a AC-1's verification command references a `d["activate"]` key in criteria_routing.json (`assert "project.review-phase-boundaries" in d["activate"]`), but no other child, the parent's AC-5, or 9447's AC-1 reference an `activate` key — they all access only `d["code_review"]["project.review-phase-boundaries"]`. This introduces a conflicting assumption about the criteria_routing.json schema that is unique to 225a.
scenarios: If the criteria_routing.json schema uses presence in the `code_review` block as the activation mechanism (no separate `activate` list), 225a's AC-1 verification command throws KeyError and fails even when the criterion is correctly configured — blocking story closure on a spurious schema mismatch
If `exec` uses a different casing or field name than `AGENT`, 225a AC-1 fails while parent AC-5 and 9447 AC-1 pass, creating an irreconcilable closure state
findings.suggested_fix: Update 225a AC-1 to match the schema used by parent and 9447: `r=json.load(open(".rebar/criteria_routing.json"))["code_review"]["project.review-phase-boundaries"]; assert r["exec"] == "AGENT" and r["block_threshold"] == 0.90 and r["blocking_enabled"] is False`. If an `activate` list is genuinely required, add it to parent AC-5 and 9447 AC-1 as well.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Generalize all AC verification commands to work across POSIX and Windows virtualenv layouts (e.g., use `python -m pytest` and `python -m rebar` invocations, or document a platform-specific path substitution rule).
evidence: `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py`
`.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_review_phase_boundaries.py`
`.venv/bin/rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries --runs 3 --output json`
`.venv/bin/python -c 'import json; r=json.load(open(".rebar/criteria_routing.json"))...'`
Python virtualenv on Windows: scripts are placed at `.venv\Scripts\python.exe` and `.venv\Scripts\pytest.exe`, not `.venv/bin/`.
findings.finding: All five AC verification commands use POSIX-style virtualenv paths (`.venv/bin/pytest`, `.venv/bin/rebar`, `.venv/bin/python`). On Windows, Python virtualenvs place scripts under `.venv\Scripts\` (not `.venv/bin/`), making every AC command non-executable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five `- [ ]` bullets referencing `.venv/bin/pytest`, `.venv/bin/rebar`, and `.venv/bin/python`
scenarios: A Windows developer (Windows platform cell of the support matrix) tasked with verifying the ACs runs `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` and receives a 'No such file or directory' / 'The system cannot find the path specified' error for every AC check, making all five acceptance criteria unverifiable on that platform without manual path translation.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `.venv/bin/pytest` with `python -m pytest`, `.venv/bin/rebar` with `python -m rebar` (or `rebar` if installed on PATH), and `.venv/bin/python -c '...'` with `python -c '...'` throughout the AC bullets, relying on the active virtualenv's PATH rather than a hard-coded POSIX script directory.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one automated check — e.g., a pytest assertion or lint rule — in test_code_review_project_criteria.py or test_project_review_phase_boundaries.py that verifies no hardcoded reference to 'project.review-phase-boundaries' (or any specific project criterion id) exists in rebar core source files.
evidence: Parent SC-1: 'A net-new project code-review criterion executes end to end through all four passes without a project-specific branch in rebar core'
2c14 scope: 'Reuse plan review's runner-side dynamic fan-in pattern without adding a workflow DSL field or a hard-coded project criterion id'
225a Dependency section: 'This story must not add a core special case to bypass that seam'
No child AC contains a grep, static-analysis, or test assertion that verifies no hardcoded project criterion ID exists in core source files
container pairing: parent + 225a-323c-a9f6-436b/2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02/9447-b31c-e0b7-446d
findings.finding: Parent SC-1 requires the criterion to execute 'without a project-specific branch in rebar core', but no child contains an automated test or verification command that asserts the absence of a hardcoded `project.review-phase-boundaries` special case in rebar core code. The constraint is stated as a design principle in 2c14 ('project-id-agnostic') and as an implementation constraint in 225a's Dependency section, but neither provides a measurable, machine-checkable verification in the same terms as SC-1.
scenarios: A developer implementing 225a inadvertently adds a `if criterion_id == 'project.review-phase-boundaries'` branch in core to work around a seam issue; no test catches it; the completion verifier must manually inspect the diff to satisfy SC-1
The absence-of-special-case property degrades silently over time since no regression test enforces it
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 2c14's test file a test such as: `def test_no_hardcoded_project_criterion_id(): import pathlib; core_src = pathlib.Path('src/rebar/core').read_text() if pathlib.Path('src/rebar/core').is_file() else ''.join(p.read_text() for p in pathlib.Path('src/rebar/core').rglob('*.py')); assert 'review-phase-boundaries' not in core_src`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (or extend AC #2) that verifies the runtime documentation deliverable of `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` — e.g., assert the file exists at its expected path, or that a `--help` / `rebar docs` invocation surfaces the new seam behavior.
evidence: Scope: 'deliver dynamic fan-in, repository-effective Pass-3 routing, project eval resolution, compatibility coverage, and the runtime documentation'
AC #1 verifies closure via `rebar list` and `git log` — no documentation check
AC #2 verifies seam behavior via `pytest tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` — no documentation check
ABSENCE: no AC references a documentation file path, a `--help` output, or any documentation artifact
findings.finding: The Scope section names 'runtime documentation' as one of five explicit deliverables of story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02`, but no acceptance criterion verifies that this documentation exists, is correct, or covers the described content. AC #1 only checks ticket closure and commit presence; AC #2 covers only the seam's runtime behavior via pytest. The documentation deliverable has no covering criterion.
findings.location: Scope, story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02`; Acceptance Criteria section (all five ACs)
scenarios: An implementer ships the seam fix and closes the story without writing any runtime documentation; all ACs pass green because none of them gate on documentation content or existence.
A reviewer approving the PR has no automated signal that documentation was updated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'The runtime documentation for the generic integration seam exists at `<expected path>` and describes dynamic fan-in, Pass-3 routing, and eval resolution; verify with `ls <path>` or equivalent doc-lint step.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what 'runtime documentation' covers in story 2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02: specify whether it includes a criterion-authoring guide (covering `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled` config keys and `criteria_routing.json` schema), and whether ADRs 0015/0016/0017 need an amendment note now that the seam is both fixed and end-to-end tested; add an AC that verifies at least one doc artifact exists.
evidence: Plan states 'deliver dynamic fan-in, repository-effective Pass-3 routing, project eval resolution, compatibility coverage, and the runtime documentation' without specifying what runtime documentation means or what it covers.
Plan introduces new config keys `block_threshold: 0.90` and `blocking_enabled: false` in `criteria_routing.json` with no pointer to where these are documented for future criterion authors.
ADRs 0015, 0016, 0017 are cited as the design basis for the extension seam; it is unspecified whether they need amendment after the seam bug is fixed and the pattern is first successfully exercised.
ABSENCE: No acceptance criterion verifies that a doc artifact or ADR exists post-completion.
findings.finding: The plan lists 'runtime documentation' as a deliverable of story 2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02 but never defines what that documentation covers or what form it takes. Specifically, it is unclear whether 'runtime documentation' includes: (a) the `criteria_routing.json` schema for the new `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled` config keys, (b) a project-criterion authoring guide explaining how future teams create and configure a `project.*` criterion end-to-end, or (c) any update to ADRs 0015/0016/0017 to reflect the now-complete seam. Without this, future project criterion authors have no navigable guide, and the architectural pattern introduced by this epic is undocumented outside the plan itself.
findings.location: Scope item 1 ('deliver … the runtime documentation') and Scope item 2 ('Author the project criterion')
scenarios: A second team wants to add their own `project.*` code-review criterion and has no documented authoring workflow or config schema to follow, relying solely on reading the first criterion's source code.
An ADR reviewer finds that ADRs 0015/0016/0017 still describe the seam as a design intent rather than a verified, tested contract, making the ADR corpus misleading after the fix lands.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC line (or Scope item) requiring a new or amended ADR / CLAUDE.md entry that records (a) the architectural choice to express phase-boundary invariants as a project-owned criterion rather than a core packaged criterion, and (b) the `suggested_fix` grandfathering policy and its planned removal trigger.
evidence: 'This epic dogfoods the existing project-criteria extension described by ADRs 0015, 0016, and 0017. The boundary policy remains a project-owned criterion rather than a packaged rebar-core criterion.' — the motivation for this placement is a new architectural decision not recorded in any named ADR.

'Existing suggested_fix support is grandfathered debt: unchanged occurrences are not findings, new or expanded Pass-1 remediation directives are findings, and the field is removed the next time its core contract is otherwise touched rather than expanding this epic into an unrelated migration.' — a new policy rule with no named doc target.
ABSENCE: No AC line, no Scope item, and no Risks/Rollout note mentions authoring or updating an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc for these decisions.
findings.finding: The plan introduces at least two novel architectural decisions — (1) the policy that project-owned criteria express phase-boundary invariants rather than core packaged criteria, and (2) the `suggested_fix` grandfathering rule (unchanged occurrences are not findings; field is removed only when the core contract is next touched) — but names no ADR or design-doc update to capture these decisions. ADRs 0015/0016/0017 are cited for the extension seam mechanism, not for these new policy choices.
findings.location: Context / Scope / 'Existing suggested_fix support is grandfathered debt' paragraph; no ADR update mentioned anywhere in the plan
scenarios: A future contributor encounters the `suggested_fix` grandfathering rule only by reading this ticket; if the ticket is closed and archived, the rationale is lost and the policy may be re-litigated or silently reversed.
A team adding a second project-owned criterion doesn't know whether to follow the 'project rather than core' pattern because no ADR records it as a deliberate architectural choice.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a fourth AC bullet: 'An ADR (or amendment to ADR-0015/0016/0017) capturing the project-vs-core criterion placement decision and the `suggested_fix` grandfathering policy is merged to `main`; verify with `git log origin/main --oneline --grep="rebar-ticket: jira-reb-1001"`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the recognition rule for code-review project prompt-ids in the eval resolver (e.g., 'a `code-review-project-<name>` id is valid iff `project.<name>` is in `effective_criteria(repo_root, gate_key="code_review")`') and document it in bug `8a4b`'s scope.
evidence: `valid = {"code-review-base", "code-review-verify"} | {f"code-review-{oid}" for oid in OVERLAY_IDS}; return prompt_id if prompt_id in valid else None` — explicit closed set, no extensibility hook.
Bug `8a4b` AC says 'loads the repository eval spec, and still rejects unknown ids' but gives no decision rule for how an activated project id is recognized as valid vs. unknown.
The plan-review analog `_criterion_id()` recognizes `project-` by checking the dotted id against the live registry; no equivalent registry-check rule is stated for the code-review arm.
findings.finding: `_code_review_prompt_id()` in `eval_solver.py` is a closed set validated against a hard-coded union of `{code-review-base, code-review-verify}` plus the 13 `OVERLAY_IDS`. A code-review project criterion (e.g., `code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries`) is not in this set and returns `None`, causing `run_case` to fall through to the agentic reviewer arm and raise `ValueError`. The plan says the seam bug must extend the eval resolver, but specifies no resolution algorithm for distinguishing a code-review project prompt-id from an unknown id.
findings.location: `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py` lines 162–176; Bug `8a4b-e8b6-1bf9-48d8` AC item 4 ('The criterion eval resolver accepts an activated code-review project id'); Story `2c14-fa5f-1228-4e02` AC item 2
scenarios: `rebar criteria eval project.review-phase-boundaries` invokes `run_case('code-review-project-review-phase-boundaries', ...)`, `_code_review_prompt_id` returns `None`, falls through to agentic arm, raises `ValueError: no eval solver for prompt`.
Implementer adds a naive `code-review-project-` prefix check without consulting `effective_criteria`, accepting unknown project ids.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add concrete `input` / `note` / `expect` skeletons for all twelve RP-F/RP-N eval cases to story `225a`'s Eval Contract section (or a linked design doc), mirroring the structure of the `project.portability` corpus.
evidence: Story `225a` Eval Contract: 'Must-fire cases cover: Pass-1 validity/impact grading; Pass-1 drop/block policy; Pass-1 remediation; Pass-2 new-finding/decision/fix leakage; an LLM/discretionary Pass 3; and Pass-4 re-adjudication or exact implementation prescription.' — six category labels only, no case text.
The analogous eval file `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml` provides a complete `note`, `input`, and `expect` for each of its 8 cases.
The criterion's Pass-1 prompt text (`code-review-project.review-phase-boundaries.md`) does not yet exist, so there is no implicit definition either.
findings.finding: The RP-F1..RP-F6 and RP-N1..RP-N6 eval case bodies are completely unspecified. Story `225a` names the six must-fire categories and six must-not-fire categories as one-liners, but provides no `input`, `note`, or `expect` content for any case — unlike the analogous `project.portability` eval corpus (which ships full `input` / `note` / `expect` fields for all 8 cases). An implementer must independently invent all 12 case bodies, creating a high risk that the corpus fails to discriminate the boundary violations it is supposed to catch.
findings.location: Story `225a-323c-a9f6-436b` — Eval Contract section; AC item 3 (`RP-F1..RP-F6` / `RP-N1..RP-N6`); compare `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml`
scenarios: Two implementers each author the 12 corpus cases independently and produce corpora with different discriminative power, making the 'recall 1.0 / false-accept 0.0' operator-attest in story `225a` AC item 4 non-reproducible.
RP-N6 ('documenting a forbidden pattern or unchanged grandfathered debt') is the highest false-positive risk; without a concrete example in the spec, the criterion is likely to fire on it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the operative-vs-descriptive instruction classification rule (e.g., imperative verb + subject + phase attribution = operative; past-tense/conditional/example-labeled text = descriptive) in story `225a`'s prompt contract section so the guard can be implemented and tested against RP-N5/RP-N6.
evidence: Story `225a` Scope: 'Guards that distinguish operative instructions from documentation or negative examples, require identifiable phase ownership, and abstain when ownership is unclear.' — the guard is required but the distinguishing rule is not specified.
The risk section: 'require an operative instruction, identifiable phase ownership, negative-example suppression, and abstention when ownership is unclear' — same absence.
No existing rubric prompt in `.rebar/prompts/` was found that defines a reusable instruction-vs-description classification rule the new prompt could reference.
Must-not-fire case RP-N6 ('documenting a forbidden pattern or unchanged grandfathered debt') is exactly the case where this guard must fire correctly, yet its triggering conditions are unstated.
findings.finding: The 'instruction-vs-description' and 'negative-example' suppression guards are required in the prompt by story `225a`'s AC item 2 and Risk section, but no decision rule or schema defining how the Pass-1 finder distinguishes an operative instruction from a documentation passage or a negative example is specified anywhere in the plan. This is a processing-protocol gap: the prompt must implement a guard whose classification rule is entirely unanchored.
findings.location: Story `225a-323c-a9f6-436b` — Scope ('Guards that distinguish operative instructions from documentation or negative examples'); Risks section ('require an operative instruction, identifiable phase ownership, negative-example suppression'); AC item 2
scenarios: The prompt author uses a heuristic like 'if the word "must" appears, it is operative' — resulting in false positives on negative-example prose containing 'must NOT'.
The guard is absent entirely, causing the criterion to fire on every rubric document that explains what a phase boundary violation looks like (a pink-elephant failure).

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
